data.title: First Horizon: Undervalued With Dividends And Robust Share Buybacks
data.description:
Regional banks remain a good value in today’s market, especially as many remain meaningfully undervalued compared to megabanks like Bank of America Corporation (BAC) and JPMorgan Chase & Co. (JPM). Regional banks have a smaller asset base than large banks, meaning that it doesn’t take as much growth to move their bottom lines.

This brings me to First Horizon Corporation (FHN), which currently sports an attractive P/E multiple with solid business results. At the current price of $24.12, FHN carries a forward P/E of 11.35 and pays a well-covered 2.5% dividend yield, as shown below.

In this article, I'm highlighting FHN, including recent business results, and discussing what makes it an attractive value and income play for potentially strong total returns, so let’s get started!

Why FHN?

First Horizon Corporation is a leading regional financial services company that’s headquartered in Memphis, Tennessee, with a legacy dating back to 1864. It serves individual consumers, businesses, and institutions through its banking subsidiary, First Horizon Bank, across the southeastern U.S. At present, FHN has over 400 banking centers and manages over $80 billion in total assets.

In addition to traditional banking services, FHN also provides specialized capabilities such as fixed income, brokerage services, and treasury management solutions that help businesses optimize liquidity and cash flow.

FHN recently demonstrated strong Q4 2025 results, with adjusted net income growing by 15% YoY to $968 million. This was driven by healthy deposit base growth and high ROTCE (return on tangible common equity) of 15.0%. Notably, ROTCE accelerated during the second half of the year, exceeding the full-year ROTCE of 14.2%.

FHN’s period-end loan and deposits grew by 3% from the prior year. FHN’s rate paid on interest-bearing deposits declined by 25 basis points sequentially to 2.53%. This downward repricing on deposit rates, combined with loan growth, contributed to NII growth of $2 million on a sequential QoQ basis. As shown below, deposit growth during Q4 was driven by an inflow into indexed deposits. Indexed deposit accounts are those in which the principal is guaranteed, and interest earned is linked to the performance of a specific market index.

At the same time, FHN saw 2.6% YoY growth in its loan portfolio as of the end of 2025, driven by more loans to mortgage companies (due to higher refinancing volume). Moreover, yields on market-based commercial real estate loans for 2025 originations improved 34 basis points YoY, reflecting better pricing discipline and a favorable loan mix. As shown below, both net interest income and net interest margin expanded in the second half of 2025 due to lower deposit rates.

Credit quality remains stable, with a net charge-off percentage rate of 0.19%, which is consistent with the 0.17% to 0.22% rate over the trailing 4 quarters. At the same time, FHN’s non-performing loans declined by $5 million over the trailing 4 quarters to $604 million, and allowance for credit losses declined by 14 basis points over the same timeframe to 1.31% of the total loan book. This signals a higher management confidence in the total loan book.

Looking ahead, management is guiding for healthy 3% to 7% revenue growth during 2026. Loan growth is guided to grow in the mid-single digits, with mortgage, commercial & industrial, and commercial real estate segments as key areas of focus. This is supported by a 5% uptick in home sales during December, which was the highest in 2 years.

Recent declines in mortgage rates since Trump announced a $200 billion mortgage-buying plan strengthen the growth outlook for FHN’s loan and mortgage business. As shown below, the 30-year mortgage rate currently sits at the lowest point in over a year at 6.06% at present.

Importantly, FHN maintains a sound balance sheet with a common equity tier 1 ratio of 10.64%, which is well exceeding the 4.5% minimum requirement. FHN currently yields 2.5%, and the dividend is very well covered by a 32% payout ratio based on $1.89 in adjusted EPS for the full-year 2025.

While FHN’s dividend rate has been flat since 2020, management is returning capital to shareholders through share buybacks as well. This is reflected by $894 million in buybacks during 2025, resulting in a 3.2% reduction in the share count.

Lastly, I find value in FHN at the current price of $24.12 with a forward P/E of 11.35, sitting well below its historical P/E of 16.3, as shown below. FHN’s valuation also sits slightly below the 11.6 forward P/E of the sector median and below the 12.3 and 14.5 forward P/E of larger peers BAC and JPM.

At the current price, FHN carries an attractive 8.8% earnings yield, which it can obtain with share buybacks. The market also doesn’t seem to be giving FHN enough credit for the positive forward outlook and solid execution, particularly in Q4 and the second half of last year, as noted earlier.

Analysts estimate 5-10% annual EPS growth over the next two years. This, combined with capital returns including dividends and share buybacks as well as a potential return to mean valuation, could result in total returns in the low to mid-teens from here. Notably, SA’s Quant currently rates FHN as a ‘Strong Buy’ with a score of 4.6 out of 5.

Risks to FHN include sensitivity to interest rates. As such, declines in interest rates could lead to margin compression on loan investments if it’s unable to offset that with lower rates on deposits. Plus, while net charge-offs remain low, a deteriorating credit environment could lead to more consumer stress and a higher charge-off rate. The commercial segment could also see headwinds from tariffs, which could result in lower demand for borrowing.

Investor Takeaway

First Horizon Corp. offers an attractive mix of value, income, and disciplined execution at the current valuation. FHN has improved profitability, stable credit quality, and a solid capital position. It’s also benefiting from easing deposit costs and steady loan growth.

The stock trades at a discount to both its own history and larger peers. It also pays a well-covered dividend and is returning capital to shareholders through robust share buybacks. FHN is positioned to deliver low- to mid-teens total returns through EPS growth and capital returns, along with potential for a return to its mean valuation. Considering all the above, I rate FHN as a ‘Buy’.

data.title: Top 10 AI Stocks For 2026
data.description:
AI Demand Boom

The global artificial intelligence market is expected to soar by a CAGR of 30.6% to exceed $3 trillion by 2033, fueled by big tech AI investments, broader adoption across industries, and an explosion in data center requirements. Companies that provide AI software, hardware, and services are well-positioned to capitalize on the megatrend and deliver significant profitable growth, along with companies that are able to exploit the technology to boost capabilities, streamline operations, increase productivity, and cut costs.

AI's long-term upside may still be in its early stages. Demand for compute continues to outpace supply, constrained by infrastructure and power limitations, Blackstone said in a research report:

"AI is the most consequential force shaping the global economy today. Adoption is happening at unprecedented speed, with over 1 billion monthly active users of ChatGPT - up from fewer than 200 million just two years ago. While overall adoption has been rapid, the impact on productivity and margins is still in early stages, with far greater long-term potential. This is fueling major investment in the infrastructure that makes it all possible, from data centers and chips to power grids and connectivity."

AI stocks have outperformed the broader market and tech sector over the past year, led by chipmakers, hyperscaler suppliers, and electric vehicle manufacturers. Performance has been supported by easing interest rates and a stable economic backdrop, which have helped sustain investor appetite for growth-oriented stocks. Positive investor sentiment toward the AI theme and robust corporate earnings helped drive momentum through a brief pullback tied to valuation concerns.

Global X Artificial Intelligence & Technology ETF: 1Y Price Performance

Tech firms leading the earnings growth charge have benefited from rising prices, as demand for AI infrastructure has outpaced supply for components such as memory chips and advanced networking hardware. The same firms are projecting strong earnings growth into 2026, driven by sustained AI capex spending and improved demand visibility.

How I Picked the Top AI Stocks for 2026

SA Quant selected 10 stocks well-positioned to ride the AI boom. The stocks are pulled from the top four largest AI and Robotic ETFs by assets under management. The professional managers' vetting process helps ensure holdings are relevant, investable, and aligned with the AI theme. We then ran the stocks from the ETFs through the SA Quant system, selecting the stocks with the strongest quant ratings. This list was created by screening holdings from major AI-focused ETFs. The list includes AI chipmakers, infrastructure builders, and software providers with strong fundamentals, momentum, and earnings growth upside. The SA Quant system gathers over 100 metrics for each stock and grades them against sector peers across five factors: Growth, Profitability, Momentum, EPS Revisions, and Valuation.

1. Lumentum Holdings Inc. (LITE)

  • Market Capitalization: $24.34B

  • Quant Rating: Strong Buy

  • Sector: Information Technology

  • Industry: Communications Equipment

  • Quant Sector Ranking (as of 1/16/2026): 2 out of 533

  • Quant Industry Ranking (as of 1/16/2026): 1 out of 38

Lumentum was picked as one of my top stocks with high 2026 earnings growth targets and is a recent addition to the PRO Quant Portfolio. AI-fueled earnings growth and market-crushing momentum have made Lumentum the top quant-rated stock in the Communications Equipment industry and Technology Sector.

Lumentum makes lasers, receivers, switches, and other components for fiber-optic networks, with about 60% of revenue deriving from AI and cloud infrastructure applications. AI-powered demand for data center laser chips and optical transceivers from hyperscale customers drove revenue up by 58% in Q1 of FY 2026 to a quarterly record of $533.8M. Needham analyst Ryan Koontz cited Lumentum's investments in expanding capabilities amid the "insatiable" global demand for lasers:

"LITE is confident its aggressive capacity expansion investments will enable it to remain the largest commercial laser supplier to the cloud industry," Koontz said in a note. "Its products continue to see global shortages and the gap in 2026 appears even larger than 2025… [and] we expect Lumentum will deliver improved results across top line, margins, income, and cash flow, and shares should continue to trend higher as major segments hit stride."

Lumentum's rate of investment is reflected in a capex/sales ratio of 12%, which is impressive relative to the tech sector median of 2.3%. Surging earnings over the past year and strong forward growth undergird an A+ Growth Grade. According to consensus estimates, Lumentum earnings are expected to grow by 177% in FY26. Sell-side analysts are increasingly bullish on Lumentum's outlook, evidenced by 17 upward earnings revisions in the last 90 days to no downward revisions. LITE is set to report earnings on 2/3/2026, facing a consensus EPS growth target of +228%.

Although high earnings multiples are driving a D+ valuation grade, Lumentum's PEG of 0.99 represents a 41% discount to the sector. PEG is an important valuation metric as it combines earnings and growth. Explosive momentum, earnings growth, and earnings growth forecasts fueled by soaring laser demand make Lumentum a top AI stock for 2026.

2. Micron Technology, Inc. (MU)

  • Market Capitalization: $378.88B

  • Quant Rating: Strong Buy

  • Sector: Information Technology

  • Industry: Semiconductors

  • Quant Sector Ranking (as of 1/16/2026): 1 out of 533

  • Quant Industry Ranking (as of 1/16/2026): 1 out of 69

#1 on my Top 10 Stocks for 2026, Micron is a leading AI chipmaker, dominant in High-Bandwidth Memory (HBM), which is crucial for AI accelerators like NVIDIA Corporation's (NVDA) Blackwell and AMD's Instinct series. Micron has absolutely crushed the sector and industry peers over the past 12 months, showcases stellar fundamentals, and is trading at a steep discount.

Micron 1Y Price Performance vs. Industry Peers

Micron projects the HBM total addressable market to reach $100B by 2028, a CAGR of 40%, two years earlier than previous forecasts. Micron revenue surged 57% YoY to $13.6B in Q1 2026, driven by outsized AI data center memory demand from hyperscaler customers, product mix, and higher pricing. Micron has sold out of its HBM supply through 2026 and expects to set records in revenue, gross margin, earnings, and free cash flow this fiscal year. After the financial results, KeyBanc analysts said a long-term shortage in AI/data center memory is driving a "supercycle" that Micron is well-positioned to capitalize on.

Micron's pricing power has fueled surging revenue, earnings, and operating income over the past year, driving an A+ Growth Grade, bolstered by a +50% EPS forward long-term growth rate (3-5Y CAGR). Exceptional operating margins supported by higher pricing and strong cost execution reinforce an A+ Profitability Grade that includes an impressive capex/sales ratio of 42%.

EBITDA is an important metric for companies with heavy capex investments, as it offers a clearer view of core operating profitability by stripping out financing, tax, and depreciation effects. Micron's EBITDA is a robust 52% vs. a sector median of 10%. Micron has 29 upward earnings revisions in the last 3 months to no downward revisions, and FY26 EPS is projected to grow by over 290% and revenue by 100%.

Despite the surging momentum, Micron has attractive valuation multiples relative to earnings, core profitability, and cash flow, and its PEG represents an 87% discount to the sector. A member of Alpha Picks and PQP, Micron offers strong fundamentals at an attractive price for investors seeking high-quality stocks leveraged to the AI boom.

3. Ciena Corporation (CIEN)

  • Market Capitalization: $34.34B

  • Quant Rating: Strong Buy

  • Sector: Information Technology

  • Industry: Communications Equipment

  • Quant Sector Ranking (as of 1/16/2026): 5 out of 533

  • Quant Industry Ranking (as of 1/16/2026): 2 out of 38

Ciena Corporation is an international optical networking company with an expanding target market as AI-fueled bandwidth consumption accelerates networking requirements. As hyperscalers race to scale AI infrastructure, Ciena sits at the center of the connectivity buildout, supplying the high-speed networking equipment and fiber-optic technologies that link data centers, clusters, and global cloud footprints.

For Q4 2025, CIEN EPS of $0.91 beat by $0.14, while revenue grew 20% to $1.35B, driven by routing and switching and global services performance. Full-year revenue rose nearly 20% to $4.77B while the company generated record annual orders of $7.8B, resulting in a record backlog heading into 2026. A substantial portion of the backlog consists of cloud service providers, including hyperscalers, and CIEN sees indications that strong demand will continue into 2027 and beyond. According to consensus estimates, CIEN earnings are projected to grow by 102%, and the stock has 15 upward earnings revisions and no down revisions in the last 3 months.

CIEN has generated solid levered free cash flow in the past year, which has soared by over 48% YoY. Ciena's A- Growth Grade rests on explosive growth in earnings, revenue, and operating income, along with an EPS forward long-term growth rate of 43%. Although Ciena is trading at a high premium to the sector, its PEG ratio is at a 38% discount to the sector. Another PQP pick, Ciena is a Strong Buy stock with surging momentum and profitable growth positioned at the center of the AI infrastructure megatrend.

4. General Motors Company (GM)

  • Market Capitalization: $75.85B

  • Quant Rating: Strong Buy

  • Sector: Consumer Discretionary

  • Industry: Automobile Manufacturers

  • Quant Sector Ranking (as of 1/16/2026): 3 out of 491

  • Quant Industry Ranking (as of 1/16/2026): 1 out of 29

The #1 quant-rated automaker stock, GM, has made strides in leveraging AI to enhance in-vehicle connectivity, safety, and hands-free driving. GM has crushed the sector and market in price performance in the past year, getting a lift after reporting that all four of its brands recorded an annual sales increase for the second consecutive year.

GM 1Y Price Performance vs. Consumer Discretionary Sector (XLY)

GM has posted solid growth in 2025 by successfully navigating headwinds from tariffs and the expiration of EV tax credits, but the auto giant still sees electric vehicles as "the end game," even if it takes longer without the incentives. Wedbush analyst Dan Ives is bullish on GM's future growth:

"We believe Barra & Co are prepared for all options on the table as it balances its ICE and EV strategies and see this company embarking down a major path of growth ahead… with GM looking to re-calibrate around high-demand, high-margin business, we believe the company is taking the right approach to navigate the EV storm."

GM's raw cash from operations of $24.21B far exceeds the sector median for an A+ Profitability Grade. GM is set to report earnings on 1/27/2026, with Q4 2025 EPS expected to surge by 17%, and has 24 upward earnings revisions for FY25 to 1 down revision.

GM is trading at a mere 7.8x earnings with a PEG of ~0.70, marking a 57% discount to the sector, although a low dividend yield has weighed on its valuation grade. With explosive momentum and healthy cash flow generation, GM offers solid exposure to AI advancements in the auto sector.

5. Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company Limited (TSM)

  • Market Capitalization: $1.39T

  • Quant Rating: Strong Buy

  • Sector: Information Technology

  • Industry: Semiconductors

  • Quant Sector Ranking (as of 1/16/2026): 9 out of 533

  • Quant Industry Ranking (as of 1/16/2026): 2 out of 69

A major supplier to Nvidia and Apple (AAPL), TSMC dominates the global semiconductor foundry market, with its share rising from 66% to 72% through Q3 2025. Capitalizing on this momentum, the AI chipmaker's stock soared over 60% in the past year, pushing TSMC into the trillion-dollar club by mid-2025.

TSMC stock rose sharply last week after a double Q4 earnings beat fueled a global AI chip rally. TSMC reported $33.73B in quarterly revenue (+25.5%), sparked by AI-related demand for leading-edge process technologies, while EPS of $3.14 beat by $0.16. Full-year revenue grew by 35.9% to $122.42B.

Shares were also bolstered by reports that the U.S. and Taiwan are nearing a new trade deal that calls for TSMC building five more facilities in Arizona, making the company's long-term outlook even stronger. Sell-side analysts are already projecting TSMC revenue to climb from $120B in FY25 to more than $240B by FY29. Huge year-over-year growth in revenue and core profitability, along with a strong long-term EPS growth rate, drives a top-notch Growth Grade. An EBITDA margin of 68% highlights its A+ Profitability Grade, bolstered by exceptional return on equity and capital.

TSMC offers a dividend yielding about 1.00% with a 5Y growth rate of 12.3% and 21 consecutive years of payments. At roughly 23x earnings, TSMC is trading at an attractive price, and a PEG of 0.80 represents a 52% discount to the sector. A company with a dominant position on the semiconductor landscape, TSMC offers a solid opportunity for investors seeking large-cap AI exposure.

6. Hut 8 Corp. (HUT)

  • Market Capitalization: $6.19B

  • Quant Rating: Strong Buy

  • Sector: Information Technology

  • Industry: Application Software

  • Quant Sector Ranking (as of 1/16/2026): 16 out of 533

  • Quant Industry Ranking (as of 1/16/2026): 1 out of 176

The top quant-ranked Application Software stock, HUT, is a leading operator of Bitcoin mining and energy infrastructure operations in North America, with a development pipeline of around 12,300 MW that continues to grow. Hut's strategic pivot to an infrastructure-first model has it well-positioned for future growth, supported by pipeline expansion, a $1.6B Bitcoin reserve, and a significant stake in American Bitcoin.

Showcasing an A+ Momentum Grade, the stock surged in December after unveiling a 15-year, $7B lease with Fluidstack for 245 MW of IT capacity. StoneX Benchmark raised its price target, citing the project, which is backed by Google (GOOGL) and supports AI workloads for Anthropic:

"The transaction combined superior deal economics relative to peer deals, long-dated, investment-grade-backstopped cash flows, and multiple layers of embedded expansion optionality across three counterparties - Anthropic, Fluidstack, and Google - and we think it has positioned HUT as one of the most advantaged AI/HPC infrastructure providers in the public markets," analyst Mark Palmer wrote in a note to clients.

Hut beat Q3 2025 revenue and earnings expectations driven by rapid growth in compute-related revenue, including Bitcoin mining and AI and data center services. Hut advanced 1,530 MW of capacity from exclusivity into development during the quarter. Hut projects the portfolio has the potential to reach 2.5 GW of Energy Capacity Under Management, subject to commercialization, according to CEO Asher Genoot.

A PQP stock with blistering momentum, Hut sits at the intersection of technology and energy, backed by a deep pipeline and scalable platform positioned to benefit from rising AI power demand.

7. Celestica Inc. (CLS)

  • Market Capitalization: $36.03B

  • Quant Rating: Strong Buy

  • Sector: Information Technology

  • Industry: Electronic Manufacturing Services

  • Quant Sector Ranking (as of 1/16/2026): 14 out of 533

  • Quant Industry Ranking (as of 1/16/2026): 2 out of 18

Another top AI stock with A+ momentum, Celestica has surged over 2,300% over the past three years, during which the stock held a Buy or Strong Buy Quant Rating on approximately 92% of trading days. Celestica has transformed into an AI hyperscaler-integrated engineering partner, offering manufacturing and design capability advantages while riding steady demand from cloud clients.

Celestica 3Y Price Return

Earnings, operating profits, cash flow, and revenue surged in the past year for a top-notch Growth Grade, largely due to high performance from the Connectivity and Cloud Solutions segment. CLS is well-positioned to deliver more profitable growth in 2026, as U.S. hyperscale capex is expected to soar. Celestica is converting growth into returns efficiently, marked by an ROTC of 21% vs. the sector's 4% for a solid Profitability Grade. Looking ahead, Celestica's strategy is focused on early investment in design engineering and system-level solutions and shifting away from lower-margin, transactional work.

Celestica's strong execution has raised Wall Street expectations, reflected in a +58% Q4 EPS growth target as the company heads into its January 28 earnings report. Despite soaring momentum and robust growth, CLS trades at a 19% discount to the sector based on PEG - an attractive setup for an AI stock with strong fundamentals.

8. Credo Technology Group Holding Ltd (CRDO)

  • Market Capitalization: $26.94B

  • Quant Rating: Strong Buy

  • Sector: Information Technology

  • Industry: Semiconductors

  • Quant Sector Ranking (as of 1/16/2026): 13 out of 533

  • Quant Industry Ranking (as of 1/16/2026): 3 out of 69

A provider of connectivity solutions for AI infrastructure, cloud computing, and hyperscale networks, Credo has capitalized on the expansion of AI clusters to post strong growth in its Active Electrical Cable (AEC) and integrated circuit (IC) core product offerings.

Credo reported record quarterly results in Q2 2026, with revenue up 272% to $268M, as hyperscalers ramped up orders of the company's power-efficient cables and connectivity chips. The company projects strong revenue growth and profitability through fiscal 2026 and beyond, supported by continued growth in its core AEC and IC franchises, along with upcoming ramps of recently announced connectivity platforms. The results reflect the continued build-out of the world's largest AI training and inference clusters, CEO William Brennan explained on the earnings call:

"AI clusters are no longer measured in tens of thousands of GPUs. They're now measured in hundreds of thousands and soon millions. The scale, density and complexity of these systems are pushing every aspect of interconnect. Reliability, power efficiency, signal integrity, latency, reach and total cost of ownership have all become mission-critical. This is the challenge our customers face, and it's where Credo is uniquely positioned to deliver the solutions they need to succeed."

Credo has strong core profitability and excellent forward revenue and EBITDA growth metrics. And its growth outlook has drawn strong analyst support, with sell-side estimates calling for EPS to climb nearly 300% in FY26.

Credo's valuation is high, but the PEG of 0.78 implies a 53% discount to the sector. Credo is a fast-growing semiconductor player with strong liquidity, accelerating momentum, and a solid outlook driven by AI tailwinds.

9. AppLovin Corporation (APP)

  • Market Capitalization: $205.14B

  • Quant Rating: Strong Buy

  • Sector: Information Technology

  • Industry: Application Software

  • Quant Sector Ranking (as of 1/16/2026): 20 out of 533

  • Quant Industry Ranking (as of 1/16/2026): 2 out of 176

A key player in the digital advertising ecosystem, AppLovin provides advanced, AI-driven marketing and monetization solutions. The stock has soared over the past year, with a boost in September after being added to the S&P 500 index, and has outperformed the tech sector, delivering solid earnings growth and a string of positive surprises.

Q3 2025 revenue rose 17% to $1.41B on continued strength in gaming advertising, adoption of its MAX platform, and earlier-than-expected international expansion. Huge operating margins and return on equity underpin APP's A+ Profitability Grade, and the robust profitability is expected to keep surging based on outstanding forward EBITDA, EPS, and revenue growth metrics. The stock is trading at a high premium, although APP's explosive long-term growth estimates have led to an attractive PEG ratio.

In starting coverage on the stock, Evercore ISI said APP is the dominant ad tech platform for mobile gaming with an emerging e-commerce performance channel that could materially expand its total addressable market. APP has ample room to grow given solid underlying industry dynamics, catalysts, and superior execution, the analysts added. APP EPS is forecast to grow by 106% in FY25 and +55% in FY26. AppLovin is turning AI-driven enhancements in ad targeting and monetization into durable revenue and profits.

10. Globus Medical, Inc. (GMED)

  • Market Capitalization: $12.57B

  • Quant Rating: Strong Buy

  • Sector: Health Care

  • Industry: Health Care Equipment

Globus Medical develops medical devices focused on musculoskeletal disorders and is capitalizing on the rapid pace of AI adoption across healthcare - including software, imaging, and robotic systems - to improve precision and support complex decision-making for surgeons. According to preliminary results, GMED Q4 revenue grew 25.2% YoY to $823M, capping off a solid year that saw annual sales up 16.5% to $2.9B. The growth was driven by solid base business performance and integration of its acquisition of Nevro, a chronic pain management device specialist, CEO Keith Pfeil said in a press release:

"Fourth quarter results reflected the continued trend of growth and market share gains within US Spine, while Enabling Technologies delivered its best quarterly revenue performance to date. We look to 2026 with confidence, remaining focused on driving innovation and operational excellence, introducing a robust pipeline of new products, maintaining disciplined investment, and executing our strategy of sustained, profitable growth."

GMED has an optimistic outlook for 2026, focused on a robust pipeline of new products and executing its strategy of sustained, profitable growth. GMED's 'A' Profitability Grade rests on strong operating margins and free cash flow generation. The company also exhibits outstanding growth in earnings and return on equity, and EPS and revenue are expected to continue rising at a solid pace over the next four years. GMED has 10 upward earnings revisions in the last 90 days to zero downward revisions.

GMED showcases a PEG of 0.08, representing a staggering 88% discount to the healthcare sector, an attractive valuation supported by a pristine balance sheet and consistent growth in cash flow. GMED wraps up my top 10 AI stocks, a list of picks well positioned for leveraging the AI megatrend to deliver more growth and capital appreciation in 2026.

data.title: EMB: The Final Piece Of The All Weather Pie
data.description:
This article is about the benefits of owning the iShares J.P. Morgan USD Emerging Markets Bond ETF (EMB).

I recently wrote about the benefits of owning the iShares U.S. Treasury Bond ETF (GOVT) and the iShares TIPS Bond ETF (TIP). That was because I think the Bridgewater concept of the All Weather investment strategy is an important one for investors to think about.

As Bridgewater wrote in its The All Weather Story insight note:

Any return stream can be broken down into its component parts and analyzed more accurately by first examining the drivers of those individual parts. The price of poultry depends on the price of corn and soymeal. The price of a nominal bond can be broken down into a real yield and an inflation component. A corporate bond is a nominal bond plus a credit spread. This way of thinking laid the groundwork for constructing All Weather. If assets can be broken down into different component parts and then summed up to a whole, so too could a portfolio.

The Bridgewater All Weather investment strategy requires you to have exposure to equities, commodities, corporate credit, emerging markets credit, nominal government bonds, and inflation-linked government bonds.

GOVT and TIP give you exposure to nominal and real U.S. Government Treasury returns. The iShares 10+ Year Investment Grade Corporate Bond ETF (IGLB), which I wrote about last year, covers you for corporate credit. Commodities can be accessed by the iShares S&P GSCI Commodity-Indexed Trust ETF (GSG), which I wrote about in December 2020, and in the five years since, it has beaten the return of the S&P 500 index by 16 percentage points.

Likely, you already have a portfolio of equities, and equities are the easiest asset class for most investors to execute security selection.

So the final asset class the All Weather strategy calls for is emerging market credit, as this will perform for you when we have rising growth or when we have rising inflation. Luckily, there is a highly efficient ETF for this asset class as well.

EMBI

When I worked in emerging markets bond trading 20 years ago on the sell side, our dealing universe was essentially the bonds that were in the J.P. Morgan EMBI Global Core Index. This index covers the largest hard-currency-denominated emerging market sovereign bonds. It is the benchmark, therefore, for institutional investors who are invested in this asset class. In 2007, iShares and J.P. Morgan teamed up to create the iShares J.P. Morgan USD Emerging Markets Bond ETF. It is a very useful investment vehicle and is therefore a highly liquid ETF.

EMB

I am selecting this ETF for my DIY All Weather fund because this ETF is low-cost and liquid, and has a long-term track record and tracks what, in my mind, is a highly recognised and credible benchmark for its asset class. This ETF pays distributions monthly and at the time of writing has 674 holdings.

The ETF has an expense ratio of 0.39%, which might seem high compared to equity index trackers, but underlying emerging market bond markets are more illiquid than large-cap equities, so there's no getting around a higher expense ratio for this asset class. Still, it has a B- score from Seeking Alpha Ratings. That said, this is still cheaper than the median expense ratio of all ETFs, which is an expense ratio of 0.50%.

In fact, EMB has become such an important investment vehicle that its own Bid / Ask % Spread is just 0.01%, giving it an A+ score from Seeking Alpha ratings.

Risks

The EMB fund has an Effective Duration of 6.8 years, so I classify it in the intermediate bucket of interest rate risk. It has a three-year Equity Beta of 0.46, making it a good portfolio diversified relative to your equity holdings.

The fund has an Average Yield to Maturity of 5.93%, giving it a nice yield pick up of 203 basis points relative to GOVT. Of course, this is to compensate for the credit risk of emerging market sovereigns, relative to the United States Treasury.

The fund's holdings currently consist of 86% Sovereign bonds and 14% Agency bonds.

The iShares website provides an Excel spreadsheet with all holdings, but the top 10 specific issuing entities are as follows:

Although the reason to own this fund is to be part of an All Weather strategy, I am pleased the fund holds 3.1% in Argentina, as I am bullish on that country given President Milei's reforms there. The largest weighting, Turkey, might create some risk with The Economist saying President Recep Tayyip Erdoğan is "likely to ask parliament to call a snap election, probably in late 2027."

The fund has exposure to sovereign and/or agency bonds of all the main emerging markets. Quite literally from A to Z: Angola, Argentina, and Azerbaijan through to Zambia.

This includes a 3.3% weighting to China and 1.3% weighting to Ukraine. It is worth noting that the fund has no exposure to Russia or Venezuela.

At the end of the day, the fund's holdings are decided by what goes into the J.P. Morgan EMBI Global Core Index, and this follows clear rules around issuer size and liquidity.

Macro

The highly diversified nature of EMB means that it really is a good vehicle to capture emerging market credit risk. Emerging market credit is generally a risk-on kind of investment, doing well when the world economy is doing well, or doing well when US interest rates come down, and investors seek higher-yielding assets, and hedge funds engage in the carry trade (borrowing at low interest rates to invest at higher interest rates).

I don't have a strong view on near or medium-term emerging market credit spreads, although if you put a gun to my head, I would say I think the Federal Reserve will be dovish, and this normally supports emerging markets.

More importantly, EMB is an excellent way to allocate to the EM credit bucket in the All Weather investment strategy, given its broad exposure to the asset class, relatively low cost, and high trading liquidity. That liquidity might be reduced at the next "emerging markets crisis", which will come around sooner or later, but we are not owning this investment as a trade, but as a long-term investment.

Conclusion

I, like you, want to pick stocks for my equity allocation. For the other All Weather asset class exposures, I recommend buy and hold positions in GSG for commodities, IGLB for corporate credit, GOVT for nominal government bonds, TIP for inflation-linked government bonds, and EMB for emerging markets credit.
